text: The glass-walled meeting room is sterile and ordered, yet eerily empty. It amplifies my awareness of boundaries—between interior and exterior—and of all the latent potential within its unoccupied space. Stepping into the second scene, I’m thrust into an atmosphere buzzing with color and the energy of human curiosity, research, and invention. The words on the wall invite me to contemplate uncertainty, ambiguity, and the optimism that comes with possibility. I feel a gentle tension between isolation and connection, order and experimentation. The overall sensation is one of standing at the threshold of creative discovery, tinged with hope and the subtle unease of the unknown.
